response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience (founded an open-source geo-data platform, built web GIS applications, spoke at FOSS4G) and has real low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second with asynchronous I/O and caching, albeit in DLang rather than C++. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his professional C++ experience is limited and dated (roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project), with no Rust at all. His recent work at Google and Meta is full-stack TypeScript/Node.js product engineering, not systems-level pipeline work, and he has no navigation guidance or routing experience specifically. The domain overlap and systems aptitude make him worth a conversation only if the team is open to a C++ ramp-up.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data mapping platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec) using async I/O, fibers, and caching in a systems language (DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, Linux/devops experience, and open-source testing tooling authorship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (last professional use ~2015); no Rust experience
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles are full-stack web/product work in TypeScript, a significant distance from systems-level data pipeline engineering
